blocks.content: The morning stretched. Someone made more coffee. Someone turned on a fan. The house had that quality it gets when people have been inside it too long — slightly stale, slightly too warm, all the small irritants of other people's habits suddenly louder.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: We haven't talked about the evening. After dinner.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: We did the afternoon. But after dinner — after the argument stopped — what was everyone doing?

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: We were all here. At the table. Doing dishes.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: All of us. The whole time.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Are you sure? Because I remember getting up for wine. And going to the bathroom. People were moving around.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I mean — generally. We were all here.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Sophie, nobody tracks every minute at dinner.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: No. I know. I'm saying — after the argument stopped, between nine forty-five and when you went upstairs at ten-thirty — can any of us say exactly where everyone was every minute of that time?
Nobody answered. Which was an answer.
